task:
ATOM 3a — Play taxonomy reframe-and-reserve, GOVERNANCE/DOCS ONLY (no code, no enum deletion). This reverses ADR-0010's 2026-06-19 'five named classes' amendment and is authorized by the founder ratification recorded in this run's priority (run_166, 2026-06-20).

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Create cocoder/decisions/0028-play-taxonomy-three-axes.md (next sequential ADR). Status: Accepted. It must, as a founder-approved decision (ratified run_166, 2026-06-20):
   - Amend ADR-0010 to describe the Play taxonomy as THREE ORTHOGONAL AXES — `triggerClass`, `executionModel`, `writeScope` — plus the existing `kind` field, rather than 'five named classes'.
   - Mark the two unused trigger values `tool/API-triggered` and `interactive` as explicitly RESERVED / forward-declared, with their named committed future use: API-triggered dispatch, and interactive browser control respectively. They are declared in the contract but not yet exercised.
   - State plainly that NO enum values are deleted; this is a vocabulary/governance reframe, not a code deletion. Note that the one behavior change (a per-persona manifest guard hiding reserved values from personas) lands in a SEPARATE follow-up atom (3b), not here.
   - Follow the existing ADR file format/front-matter used by neighboring ADRs (read 0025/0026/0027 for the current template), and reference ADR-0010 and this priority.
2. Amend cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md: mark its 2026-06-19 'five named classes' amendment as superseded/amended by ADR-0028, with a one-line pointer. Do NOT rewrite 0010 wholesale — preserve its history; add the amendment marker the way living-ADR convention (ADR-0014) does.
3. Update the ADR index (cocoder/decisions/README.md) so 0028 is listed and the 0010→0028 amendment relationship is visible.
4. Align any surface that still restates the live framing as 'five named classes' to the three-axes vocabulary. ARCHITECTURE.md reportedly already uses axes (verify; small/no change). Check docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md and any persona/Play standards prose. Do NOT touch packages/** code in this atom.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- ADR-0028 exists, Accepted, correctly cross-referenced to ADR-0010 and the priority; reserved values named with their future use.
- ADR-0010 carries an amendment marker pointing to 0028; ADR index updated.
- No surface describes 'five named classes' as the current live framing (it may appear only as superseded history).
- ZERO enum/code changes: no value removed from any Play taxonomy enum; no file under packages/** modified.
- Play behavior-pinning tests remain green (this atom is docs/governance only — run them to confirm nothing regressed): the 4 Play suites under packages/core/tests/** and scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: no Play enum value removed; no manifest guard yet; behavior-pinning tests stay green; don't touch runner/daemon/persona CODE.

WRITE SCOPE: cocoder/decisions/**, ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/**. (No packages/** edits in this atom.)

Report the new ADR path, the exact lines amended in 0010 and the index, every surface you aligned, and the test command + output proving the Play suites are green.

ATOM 3b — manifest guard for reserved Play taxonomy values. This is the ONE behavior change ratified for this slice (priority's Founder Ratified Decisions, point 4). It implements the rule ADR-0028 (just landed) declares: `tool/API-triggered` (triggerClass) and `interactive` (kind) are RESERVED/forward-declared — declared in the contract but no dispatch path honors them yet — so they must NOT be advertised to personas, preventing a 'looks supported, isn't' trap.

OWNER (define the reserved set ONCE — elegance, one owner): add a single predicate, e.g. `isReservedPlay(play)` (or `playIsReserved`), that returns true when a Play is gated on a not-yet-shipped runtime path: `triggerClass === 'tool/API-triggered'` OR `kind === 'interactive'`. Put it where both consumers below can import it (packages/core/src/plays/manifest.ts or types.ts). Do NOT scatter the literal reserved values across call sites — both consumers derive from this one predicate.

TWO CONSUMERS DERIVE FROM IT:
1. `renderPlayManifest` (packages/core/src/plays/manifest.ts): filter out reserved Plays from the per-persona manifest so a persona never sees a Play it cannot actually trigger. Keep the existing rendering for non-reserved Plays byte-identical.
2. Persona request validation (packages/core/src/plays/request.ts): reject a request for a reserved Play with a clear, new rejection code (e.g. `reserved-play`) and message — so even if a persona names one directly, it cannot be requested. Place this guard consistently with the existing unknown/unauthorized/mandatory rejection ordering.

WHY BOTH: the manifest filter is the ratified behavior; the request rejection keeps the contract consistent (not-advertised AND not-requestable) so the guard can't be bypassed by naming the Play directly. They MUST share the one predicate.

DO NOT delete or narrow any enum: `PLAY_TRIGGER_CLASSES` keeps `tool/API-triggered`; `kind` keeps `interactive`. Reserved ≠ removed. No shipped Play currently declares a reserved value, so existing manifests/fixtures are unaffected — add a test fixture Play that DOES declare a reserved value to exercise the guard.

TESTS (behavior-pinning — must stay green and GAIN coverage): extend the existing Play suites under packages/core/tests/** (plays-request.test.ts for the reserved-play rejection; the manifest-render coverage — add/extend the suite that renders the manifest) to pin: (a) a reserved-value Play is omitted from `renderPlayManifest` output for an authorized caller while a normal Play for the same caller still renders unchanged; (b) a persona request for a reserved-value Play is rejected with the new code; (c) a normal (non-reserved) Play request still passes exactly as before. Do not weaken any existing assertion.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- One reserved predicate; both manifest render and request validation import it (no duplicated literal value lists).
- Reserved Play hidden from per-persona manifest; reserved Play request rejected with a clear new code; non-reserved Play behavior byte-identical.
- No enum value removed; no Play file or persona prompt changed to add a reserved value to a real shipped Play.
- All existing Play suites green PLUS the new coverage; run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and report the full pass count, and run `node scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` (must stay green).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: the four Play suites and runner behavior-pinning tests stay green; non-reserved Play rendering and request acceptance unchanged; the spine/commit/verify behavior untouched.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/** (manifest.ts, request.ts, types.ts as needed, and packages/core/tests/**). Do NOT touch cocoder/decisions/** or docs — ADR-0028 already landed.

Report: the predicate name + file, the exact edits to both consumers, the new rejection code, the test names added, and the full `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` pass count + enforcer-proof exit.
